name: Network Sniffer
description: Advanced network packet sniffer for macOS built on the Wireshark CLI (tshark). For AUTHORIZED network analysis only. Lists capture interfaces; captures live traffic to pcap files; filters by IP or domain; performs a full-network scan with protocol hierarchy, conversations and top talkers; extracts contacted domains (DNS/TLS SNI/HTTP Host); searches packet payloads; extracts cleartext credentials; runs a PII/secrets DLP scan; follows TCP streams and carves transferred files; lists HTTP transactions; surfaces TLS/cert intel; runs threat-detection heuristics (port scans, ARP anomalies, plaintext auth, beaconing); matches traffic against an IOC list; and analyzes existing pcap files. Passive capture/analysis only — no injection, spoofing or active attacks. Requires tshark (brew install --cask wireshark).
author: custom

examples.prompt: Capture all traffic to and from example.com for 30 seconds
Run a full network scan for 60 seconds
Which domains is my machine contacting?
Search captured traffic for the word password
Look for cleartext credentials on the network
Scan the network for passwords, personal data and other sensitive information
List the HTTP requests and responses in /tmp/dump.pcap
Show TLS certificates and weak protocol versions for 60 seconds
Carve the HTTP files transferred in /tmp/dump.pcap

action.description: Action to perform: 'interfaces' (list capture interfaces), 'capture' (live capture to a pcap file), 'target' (capture/filter traffic for a specific IP or domain), 'scan' (full-network scan with protocol hierarchy, conversations and top talkers), 'domains' (extract contacted domains from DNS/TLS SNI/HTTP), 'search' (search payloads for a string or display filter), 'credentials' (extract cleartext credentials), 'sensitive' (PII/secrets DLP scan), 'analyze' (analyze an existing pcap file), 'http' (list HTTP transactions), 'tls' (TLS/certificate intel and weak-protocol flags), 'threats' (heuristic detection: port scans, ARP anomalies, plaintext auth, beaconing), 'follow' (follow a TCP stream or carve transferred files via 'extract'), 'ioc' (match traffic against an indicator list in 'iocFile'), 'cleanup' (delete temporary captures and carved objects)

interface.description: Network interface to capture on, e.g. 'en0'. Optional; if empty tshark picks the first non-loopback interface. Use action 'interfaces' to list valid names.

target.description: An IP address or domain name to capture/filter. Required for action 'target'. Domains are resolved to their IPs and matched in both directions.

duration.description: Live capture duration in seconds. Defaults to 60 when neither duration nor packetCount is set. Hard maximum 600.

packetCount.description: Maximum number of packets to capture. Defaults to 5000 when neither duration nor packetCount is set. Hard maximum 200000.

captureFilter.description: Optional libpcap/BPF capture filter, e.g. 'tcp port 80' or 'host 1.2.3.4'. Applied at capture time.

displayFilter.description: Optional Wireshark display filter, e.g. 'http' or 'dns.qry.name contains example'. Applied when reading packets.

searchTerm.description: Substring to search for inside packet payloads (used by action 'search'; optional extra filter for 'sensitive').

pcapFile.description: Path to a pcap/pcapng file. For 'analyze' it is the file to read. For other actions, supplying it reads from this file instead of capturing live; supplying it on a capturing action writes the capture to this path.

streamIndex.description: TCP stream index to follow (action 'follow'). If omitted, the first stream matching searchTerm/displayFilter is followed.

extract.description: For action 'follow': protocol whose transferred objects/files should be carved out of the capture. One of 'http', 'smb', 'smb2', 'tftp', 'imf', 'dicom'. Files are written to a temp dir; run 'cleanup' to remove them.

iocFile.description: Path to a newline-separated list of indicators (IPs and/or domains; '#' lines ignored). Required for action 'ioc'.

outputFormat.description: Output format for list-style actions (search, http, tls, domains, ioc): 'json' (default), 'csv', or 'ndjson'. CSV/NDJSON emit flat rows suitable for SIEM import.
